host: Intel Core Ultra 9 275HX (Arrow Lake-HX, 24 logical cores; AVX2 + AVX-VNNI, no AVX-512), 30 GB RAM, no NVIDIA module loaded
notes: Both eliza-1-0_6b and eliza-1-1_7b bundles ship the SAME tts/ artifacts (OmniVoice-base 0.6B Q4_K_M LM + tokenizer/codec Q4_K_M); the TTS RTF is therefore identical across the two tiers — the tier difference is which text backbone feeds the chunker, not the voice model.

Batch-only path: the CLI synthesizes the whole phrase (32-step MaskGIT decode -> DAC codec decode) before writing any audio. First-audio latency == whole-phrase synthesis wall time. The libelizainference ABI v2 streaming-TTS entries (eliza_inference_tts_synthesize_stream / *_supported) are honest stubs (return 0) until W7's fused streaming decoder lands — there is no sample-streaming yet.
MaskGIT LM forward dominates: ~90% of wall time (e.g. 1-word: 7.5s of 8.3s). The codec/DAC decode (kept on CPU per the documented Metal decode-stall fallback) is NOT the RTF bottleneck on this host.

MaskGIT is a FIXED 32 demask steps regardless of phrase length; the per-step LM forward cost grows with the audio-token sequence length (T), so RTF is roughly flat (~6-9x) across short clauses to a paragraph but absolute latency scales ~linearly with phrase length.

Threading: omnivoice.cpp pins the CPU backend to hardware_concurrency()/2 = 12 threads here (src/backend.h backend_cpu_n_threads); not exposed as a CLI flag. CUDA unavailable (nvidia kernel module not loaded); no linux-x64-vulkan-fused build on disk, and llama-omnivoice-server exposes no GPU-offload flag, so no Vulkan TTS measurement was possible on this host.

Gate context: voice_rtf gate is <=0.5 (0_6b) / <=0.45 (1_7b). CPU-only is far over gate; the gate is meant for the GPU path (Apple Metal measured ~17ms/step for 'Hi.' in the fused-voice-ffi-smoke -> ~13x faster than this CPU box's ~235ms/step). Real publish RTF must come from GPU offload of the MaskGIT LM (Metal/Vulkan/CUDA) with the DAC codec staying CPU.

phrases.text: The capital of France is Paris.
I have scheduled the meeting for tomorrow at three o'clock.
Here is a short summary of the document you asked about. It covers three main points and a recommendation. Let me know if you want more detail on any of them.

summary.passesGateVulkan: not-measured (no vulkan-fused build / no GPU-offload CLI flag)
summary.passesGateCuda: not-measured (nvidia kernel module not loaded on this host)
summary.phraseCacheMitigation: cached common phrases (DEFAULT_PHRASE_CACHE_SEED / FIRST_AUDIO_FILLERS) bypass synthesis entirely -> ~0 ms first-audio; cache seed PCM ships in cache/voice-preset-default.bin (currently N=0 phrases in the staged stand-in) and EngineVoiceBridge re-prewarms at idle when a real TTS backend is present.
